body_txt: When a student is under a mentor's tutelage, the mentor's ability overshadows that of the student. This is certainly justified, since a student intends to develop skills by learning from a more experienced mentor. However, there may come a time when the student's ability surpasses their mentor's. Traditionally, this means the student completes his study and is deemed a master of his trade. This may result in the mentor becoming an Obsolete Mentor . An arrogant student, when he proves he is superior, often utters a Stock Phrase or some variation which originates the trope name: "The student has surpassed the teacher." A nice mentor will usually be very proud of his student , though perhaps with a bit of ego-stroking about his own teaching skills. If the student has surpassed his mentor but hasn't reached his full potential, his old master may tell him to find a new master who will teach the student more . On the other hand, a jealous mentor may become resentful of the student's ability, and in extreme cases, the mentor may antagonize their student. When both the teacher and the student are evil, the student often proves his superiority by killing the teacher . Direct Sub-Trope of Superior Successor . Some teachers might resort to Limiting the Potential as a way to prevent this from happening, either out of jealousy or to teach the student humility and responsibility first.
